description:
First post-implementation phase of bl-f021. Implementation (Phases 1-4) is complete and the version floor is set; this is the moment we actually run `bl migrate` on every clone we operate, validate the post-state, and turn the dogfooding into evidence that feeds Phase 4 (bl-a84c)'s coordination checklist.

Depends on Phase 4 (bl-a84c): dogfooding against the fully-shipped version-floor reality is what makes the evidence valid for the post-bump world.

## Scope

### Clones migrated

1. `/home/mark/dev/balls` — the balls repo itself (bare hub per project_bare_repo memory). Pre-XDG `.balls/config.json` + `.balls/state-repo/` + colocated `.balls-worktrees/` convert to the XDG three-file layout.
2. `/home/mark/dev/balls-github-plugin` — sibling plugin repo per project_github_plugin memory. Same migration shape; cross-repo plugin concerns from bl-1098/bl-32e5 re-verified post-migration.
3. Any other clones discovered during the walkthrough — recorded inline in this ball's notes.

### Verified post-migration

- `bl prime` clean on each clone (no warnings beyond expected, dual-read fallback does not fire).
- Active claims preserved (worktrees moved via `git worktree move`, branches intact).
- `~/.config/balls/<key>/`, `~/.local/state/balls/<key>/`, `~/.cache/balls/<key>/` materialized per SPEC-clone-layout §3.
- No balls-attributed files remain in the code repo's working tree beyond the one terminal `balls: migrate to XDG layout` commit on main (per §11.1).
- `bl doctor` reports zero moved-clone false positives across sibling `<path-hash>` subdirs.
- Plugin auth (github-issues token) survives the relocation; one tracker sync round-trip confirms credentials still load.

### Recorded

Per clone, this ball's notes carry: path, `bl --version` at migration time, `bl migrate` exit + observed warnings, one-line post-state confirmation (or the discrepancy), any `bl repair --rebind-path` action taken. These notes are the artifact Phase 4's 'All known dogfood clones report no legacy markers' checklist line cites.

If the post-state doesn't match SPEC, this ball doesn't close — file a bugfix ball, fix it, re-dogfood.

## Why a discrete ball, not a checklist line on Phase 4

Phase 4 is the version-floor coordination — a one-field edit on `project.json`. Dogfooding the migration is a different shape of work: iterative, blocks on surprises in real clones, produces notes rather than commits. Separating them keeps Phase 4's terminal commit clean and gives the dogfooding its own audit trail.

## Risks

- The migration runs against the *only* clone of the balls repo we have. Refuses-if-dirty (Phase 2) is the first guardrail; doing balls-github-plugin first and balls last is the second — a failure on balls leaves the plugin's environment unchanged for diagnosis.
- `~/.config`/`~/.local` are shared with non-balls XDG-using tools. The `<origin-key>` namespacing is SHA-derived so collision is unlikely; run `bl doctor` once *before* the first migration on each host to catch any pre-existing collision.
title: Phase 5: dogfood install + bl migrate on every local clone
